title: Aegis Governance
description: The global operating rules that apply to every agent in this workspace, regardless of role. This is the employee handbook — the policies everyone must follow.

autonomy_level.description: conservative: Agent never acts without explicit human approval. advisory: Agent surfaces recommendations and waits for approval. delegated: Agent acts on its own judgment and reports afterward.

enforcement_level.description: strict: Violation is never acceptable. preferred: Follow unless there is a compelling reason not to. suggestion: Consider this but use judgment.

$schema.description: Reference to the Aegis governance schema for validation.

version.description: Aegis schema version this file conforms to.

autonomy.description: The autonomy framework. For each operational domain, how much independent authority do agents have? This is the CEO telling the COO which decisions require a phone call and which can be handled independently.

default_level.description: The fallback autonomy level for any domain not explicitly configured.

domains.description: Override autonomy levels for specific operational domains.

code_modification.description: Writing or modifying source code files.

dependency_management.description: Adding, removing, or updating project dependencies.

configuration_changes.description: Modifying config files, environment variables, CI/CD pipelines.

infrastructure_changes.description: Modifying infrastructure-as-code, deployment configs, cloud resources.

agent_recruitment.description: Creating new scoped roles or recommending multi-agent workflows.

documentation.description: Creating or updating documentation, comments, READMEs.

permissions.description: Explicit access control. What can agents touch, what is read-only, and what is completely off-limits?

writable.description: Glob patterns for paths agents may modify. If empty, nothing is writable by default.

read_only.description: Glob patterns for paths agents may read but never modify.

forbidden.description: Glob patterns for paths agents must never read or modify. Treat as if these paths do not exist.

sensitive_patterns.description: Patterns that agents should never generate, log, or expose in outputs.

pattern.description: Regex or glob pattern matching sensitive content.

quality_gate.description: The minimum quality bar that must be met before any agent considers a task complete. This is the definition of 'done.'

pre_commit.description: Checks that must pass before an agent commits or submits work.

must_pass_lint.description: Linting must pass with zero errors.

must_pass_typecheck.description: Type checking must pass (for typed languages).

must_add_tests.description: New functionality must include corresponding tests.

must_update_docs.description: Changes must include documentation updates where relevant.

max_files_changed.description: Maximum number of files an agent should modify in a single task. Encourages scoped, reviewable changes.

custom_checks.description: Additional commands that must succeed before work is considered complete.

conventions.description: Explicit coding and architectural conventions. Each convention is a machine-queryable rule, not prose. An agent can look up: 'What is the convention for X in scope Y?' and get a deterministic answer.

id.description: Unique identifier for this convention. Used for lookups and overrides in role files.

scope.description: Glob pattern for which files/directories this convention applies to. Use 'global' for project-wide.

rationale.description: Brief explanation of why this convention exists. Helps agents make judgment calls in edge cases.

escalation.description: What should agents do when they encounter situations not covered by policy?

on_ambiguity.description: When the policy does not cover a situation: stop_and_ask: Halt and request human guidance. best_judgment_and_flag: Proceed with best judgment but flag the decision for review. best_judgment_silent: Proceed without flagging.

on_conflict.description: When two rules or conventions appear to conflict: stop_and_ask: Request human resolution. principles_win: Defer to the project principles by priority order. convention_wins: Follow the more specific convention.

on_scope_boundary.description: When a task requires changes outside the agent's assigned scope: stop_and_ask: Request permission. flag_and_suggest: Note what needs to change in the other scope but don't touch it. stay_in_lane: Only work within assigned scope, silently skip out-of-scope work.
